en.extract:
The yellow-streaked warbler (Phylloscopus armandii) is a species of leaf warbler (family Phylloscopidae). It was formerly included in the "Old World warbler" assemblage.
It breeds across much of China and migrates south to Yunnan and northern Southeast Asia. Its natural habitat is temperate forests.
It has a variety of vocalizations, with dozens of syllable types.

The '''yellow-streaked warbler''' ('''''Phylloscopus armandii''''') is a [[species]] of leaf warbler ([[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Phylloscopidae]]). It was formerly included in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage.

It breeds across much of [[China]] and migrates south to [[Yunnan]] and northern [[Southeast Asia]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is temperate [[forest]]s.

It has a variety of vocalizations, with dozens of syllable types.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Wang |first1=Mei |last2=Mei |first2=Jinjuan |last3=Liu |first3=Fanglin |date=2022-11-28 |title=Temporal Acoustic Patterns of the Oriental Turtle Dove in a Subtropical Forest in China |journal=Diversity |volume=14 |issue=12 |page=1043 |doi=10.3390/d14121043 |doi-access=free |issn=1424-2818}}</ref>
